title: PIPL Art.51 — information-security measures across PRC personal-data systems
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=PIPL, clause=Art.51, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PIPL Art.51 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PIPL Art.51 (Information security measures) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.10: PIPL Art.51 — information-security measures across PRC personal-data systems.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.10' running on Endpoint logs, crypto inventory, access control, vulnerability scanner feeds for PRC-resident systems., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Endpoint logs, crypto inventory, access control, vulnerability scanner feeds for PRC-resident systems.
spl:
index=prc_systems earliest=-7d
| eval control_family=coalesce(control_family,"unknown")
| stats count AS events count(eval(control_state="failed")) AS failures BY system_id, control_family
| eval failure_rate=round(100*failures/events,2)
| where failure_rate > 0 AND control_family IN ("encryption","access_control","vulnerability_mgmt","logging")
description: Aggregates security-control evidence across PRC-hosted systems handling personal information.
value: PIPL Art.51 mandates information-security management; the UC gives data processors Chinese-authority-facing evidence of Art.51 measures.
implementation: (1) Tag PRC-resident systems with a 'jurisdiction' field; (2) ingest control telemetry; (3) schedule this search nightly. Indexes required: `index=prc_systems`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `control_family`, `events`, `failure_rate`, `failures`, `system_id`.

references.title: Personal Information Protection Law of the PRC (PIPL)

knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Endpoint logs, crypto inventory, access control, vulnerability scanner feeds for PRC-resident systems..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag PRC-resident systems with a 'jurisdiction' field; (2) ingest control telemetry; (3) schedule this search nightly.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=prc_systems earliest=-7d
| eval control_family=coalesce(control_family,"unknown")
| stats count AS events count(eval(control_state="failed")) AS failures BY system_id, control_family
| eval failure_rate=round(100*failures/events,2)
| where failure_rate > 0 AND control_family IN ("encryption","access_control","vulnerability_mgmt","logging")
```

Understanding this SPL

**PIPL Art.51 — information-security measures across PRC personal-data systems** — PIPL Art.51 mandates information-security management; the UC gives data processors Chinese-authority-facing evidence of Art.51 measures.

Documented **Data sources**: Endpoint logs, crypto inventory, access control, vulnerability scanner feeds for PRC-resident systems. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: prc_systems.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=prc_systems, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **control_family** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by system_id, control_family** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **failure_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where failure_rate > 0 AND control_family IN ("encryption","access_control","vulnerability_mgmt","logging")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
